narrative:
DRIVER
GBP leads H4 CSM at 100 versus a D1 score of 0, a 100-point divergence that is the single most important short-term signal in this brief, with H4 risk-off regime accelerating while news flags GBP politically vulnerable and bearish. GBPUSD H4 sell signal at -4 is the cleanest expression, amplified by USDCHF inverse correlation at -0.77 and NZDUSD cluster at +0.83 confirming broad USD firmness against sterling. NZD H4 CSM at 99 versus D1 at 40 mirrors the same divergence, making NZDUSD a secondary short with NZDJPY momentum fading on H4.

MOMENTUM
Gaining: AUDJPY CMP 60, H4 momentum 75 rising sharply, D1 strong buy signal intact
Fading: EURUSD CMP 42, H4 momentum 21 falling hard; GBPUSD CMP 44, H4 momentum 25 falling hard

WATCH
BoJ commentary shift triggers USDJPY directional break, H4 momentum at 81 rising means upside continuation toward 158 handle
ECB speakers referencing data-dependency accelerate EURUSD below 1.1700, H4 sell signal already active
WTI Oil extended decline below 99.00 pressures CADJPY lower, H4 momentum at 69 stalls and reverses

themes.theme: Trump-Xi summit positive outcomes support risk appetite and USD strength
BoJ hawkish tilt and higher yields support JPY despite USD strength
US fiscal and inflation risks keep DXY range-bound despite PPI strength
ECB data-dependent approach maintains EUR weakness near 1.1700
GBP vulnerable amid political risks and fiscal concerns despite positive data
Commodity currencies AUD and NZD underperform despite risk-on sentiment
BoC mildly hawkish tone supports CAD amid oil price firmness
INR weakness driven by foreign outflows and higher oil prices
Oil and commodities volatile on supply deficit concerns and dollar strength
Risk-on sentiment from geopolitical optimism supports equities and USD

key_observation: Trump-Xi summit produces positive outcomes with mutual agreements on Hormuz shipping and soybean purchases, driving risk appetite. USD strengthens despite fiscal concerns as higher PPI supports hawkish Fed expectations. JPY paradoxically weakens against USD despite BoJ hawkish rhetoric, suggesting rate differential dominance. Commodity currencies AUD and NZD disappoint on the upside, indicating selective risk appetite favoring tech equities and USD over commodity exposure.
watch: US Retail Sales data, ECB meeting-by-meeting guidance implementation, GBP political developments, BoJ hawkish expectations validation, AUD break above 0.7280 resistance, commodity price trajectory amid oil supply deficit
